title: eragrostis stagnalis
intro: Eragrostis stagnalis, also known as bog grass or marsh love grass, is a type of perennial grass native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Eragrostis stagnalis include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and providing relief from minor skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Eragrostis stagnalis include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water or mixing them with a carrier oil.
